question: Can RMB 100B over 3 years generate positive ROI, and what are the unit economics of self-operated brand building?

findings.claim: Costco's Kirkland Signature generated $90B in sales in 2025, up from $75B in 2024 — demonstrating that private label at scale is achievable but took 30+ years
findings.data: Kirkland Signature 2025 sales: $90B (reported at Costco's 2026 Annual Meeting). 2024 sales: ~$75B. Growth: ~$15B YoY. Kirkland accounts for ~32% of Costco's total sales across 10 major categories. The brand was launched in 1995 — it took 30 years to reach $90B scale.

findings.claim: Kirkland Signature gross margins are ~15%, which is 5-8 percentage points above national brand equivalents at Costco — private label earns nearly double the margin
findings.data: Costco caps markups at 14% for national brands and 15% for Kirkland Signature. Kirkland gross margins: 15%+. National brand gross margins at Costco: ~7-10%. Kirkland products are priced 20% below national brands while maintaining higher margins. This is possible because Costco eliminates brand marketing costs and middlemen.

findings.claim: Costco's overall gross margin is 10-12%, with Kirkland's 15% being the margin expansion driver — this is the ceiling for self-operated retail economics
findings.data: Costco consolidated gross margin: 12.61% (FY2024). Kirkland at 15% pulls the average up, while national brands at ~7-10% pull it down. Costco's model works because membership fees ($4.8B+ annually) subsidize low product margins. Without a membership fee analog, a pure retail self-operated model would need higher product margins.
